title: Leg ulcer skin review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting underlying-condition context and clinician-assessed redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, severity, complication risk, and previous antibiotic exposure.
assessment.rationale: The exact leg-ulcer section supports documenting interval course, symptoms, functional effect, site, surrounding skin, warmth, spreading redness or swelling, pain, fever, underlying-condition and prior-antibiotic context without diagnosing infection.

source.title: Leg ulcer infection: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE wound, vascular, microbiology, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults with a leg ulcer, including possible or established infection and reassessment after treatment.
source.setting: Primary care, community wound care, and hospital referral assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting ulcer cause context, redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, worsening, systemic illness, disproportionate pain, comorbid risk, and reviewed microbiology. It does not diagnose infection or generate antibiotics, testing, referral, or urgency.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.3 — causes, colonisation, and infection signs

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting underlying-condition context and clinician-assessed redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, severity, complication risk, and previous antibiotic exposure.

assessment.text: Supports documenting rapid worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, previous antibiotics, microbiology sampling already performed, and results already reviewed.
assessment.rationale: The reassessment section supports recording worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, disproportionate pain, previous antibiotic exposure and microbiology results already reviewed; no test or treatment is generated.

assessment.text: Supports recording clinician-identified serious illness, diabetes, immunosuppression, lymphangitis, spreading infection, or completed referral or specialist discussion; it does not create the referral.
assessment.rationale: The referral section supports documenting clinician-discussed safety net and an arranged review or completed specialist discussion; it does not create referral or urgency.
